core_payload.content:
# Symphony Retry Entry

## 1. Dream Direction
The Dream branch imagines retry state becoming a more expressive quiet-recovery object rather than a thin timer wrapper.

## 2. Desired Future State
- lane-aware retry policy instead of one generic backoff path.
- better degraded-mode handling when one execution lane fails but another can continue.
- clearer assignment return paths so async work can still surface results or failures correctly.
- quieter operator behavior where recoverable states stay inspectable without generating chaotic noise.

## 3. Why It Matters
Dream preserves the idea that resilience should look deliberate rather than desperate. Good retry state is part of trustworthy orchestration, not just an implementation detail.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Retry Entry in the Dream branch preserves a richer quiet-recovery object for orchestration. It imagines lane-aware retry policy, degraded-mode continuation, clearer assignment return paths, calmer operator-visible recovery behavior, and more deliberate handling of recoverable failure without duplicate work. The capsule matters because trustworthy automation depends on retry semantics that feel governed rather than improvised, especially when multiple lanes, async assignments, and human review surfaces are involved across one orchestration family.
